title: Mail are: anyone used stickers?
body:
I heard dark-coloured nail polish can help minimise nail crumbling during chemotherapy (something about stopping UV light getting in and reacting with the chemo agents). 

On the one hand I like the idea of this as I want to keep my nails if at all possible; but on the other hand a dark polish means I can’t monitor any natural colour changes to my nail beds (I heard that chemo can sometimes cause them to turn black). 

So my imagination has come up with a half-way house, but I’m wondering how realistic it is: I’m thinking to use a clear nail strengthened coat directly on my nails and then use dark coloured stickers over that - to protect from in light, whilst still allowing me to peel up the corners and have a peak through the clear polish to the colour of my nail beds.  

Anyone tried this? Did it work? What did you do to minimise nails falling off/ crumbling?  I don’t start chemo for another week yet … enough time to gather together supplies. Is my sticker idea bonkers? To my mind it minimises the risks of accidentally pushing off my finger nails when rubbing in nail polish remover to take off any coloured polish in order to inspect nail beds periodically.
